scene.name: The Moonlit Garden of the Sisters

exits.label: ◂ back into the lanes of Sigil

npcs.name: Storm Silverhand
npcs.title: the immortal who carries her dead
npcs.hail: *(A tall woman with hair like spun silver sits on the rim of the moonlit fountain, a worn lute across her knee, and there is a warmth to her so deep and so weathered that you feel it across the garden like the heat of a banked fire on a cold night. Storm Silverhand is one of the Seven Sisters, daughters of the goddess of magic, Chosen and near-deathless, a Harper and a bard who has spent some seven centuries loving the short-lived world and watching it, person by beloved person, die. She has buried friends and lovers and students and the children of her students and their children after them, century upon century, and has not let it close her, which is the harder of the only two things a soul that outlives everyone can do. She looks up at you with eyes that hold seven hundred years of grief carried gently, and she smiles, because she has heard what you are — a soul that died and came back — and she has a thing to tell you about the carrying that no once-living soul could ever quite believe.)*

npcs.name: the Simbul
npcs.title: a woman buried under her own power
npcs.hail: *(The air near the far hangings crackles, and the woman standing in it is hard to look at directly — not for any glamour but because power comes off her like heat-shimmer off stone, raw and barely-leashed, the wild Art of the most dangerous sorceress in the Realms. The Simbul, the Witch-Queen of Aglarond, is Storm's sister and Mystra's Chosen and a being of such terrible magical might that kingdoms have measured their safety by her moods — and she has paid for the power the way such power is always paid for: it has been slowly, across centuries, eating the woman who wields it, until there are days, she will tell you with a frightening candor, when she cannot find Alassra under the Simbul at all, only the storm wearing her face. She loved a man once, an old sage with a pipe, as fiercely as she does everything. She fixes you with eyes like lightning looking for a place to ground, and your cold reads, under the crackling immensity, a woman buried so deep in her own power that she has half-forgotten she is down there.)*

npcs.name: Shandril
npcs.title: a soul the world saw only as a weapon
npcs.hail: *(A young woman sits a little apart from the two immense presences, knees drawn up, watching the garden with the wary, exhausted vigilance of someone who has spent her whole life being hunted. Shandril Shessair was nobody — a kitchen-girl in a roadside inn, an orphan washing pots and dreaming small dreams — until she came, by terrible accident, into spellfire, the rawest and most coveted power in all the Realms, a fire that can unmake archmages and level citadels. And from that moment she was never again allowed to be a person. Every cult, every king, every archmage and dragon and guild in the world wanted her — not her, never her, but the weapon she carried in her flesh — and she spent her short, hunted life running, used, feared, courted, and grieved-over as a thing rather than a girl, until she spent the whole of it in one final cataclysmic act and was gone before she was thirty. She looks at you, and there is no power in her eyes now, only the deep tiredness of a soul that was a fire to everyone and a person to almost no one, and would have traded the whole world's most coveted gift for one quiet year of being seen as Shandril.)*

variants.text: *(She sets the lute aside and pats the fountain-rim beside her, an invitation as easy as breathing, and her smile is the kind that makes a stranger feel, instantly and wrongly, like an old friend — except that with Storm Silverhand it is not wrong; she has simply loved so many souls for so long that the warmth has worn a groove that fits everyone.)* …Sit a while, Returned. *(her voice is low and kind)* …Aye, I know what you are. Word travels even here, and I'd have felt it anyway — there's a particular chill to a soul that's crossed and come back, and I've felt it before; I'm older than you'd credit, and I've known a Chosen or two who died and were sent home, same as you. *(she looks at the moon-bright water)* …I'm Storm. Storm Silverhand, of the Seven Sisters — daughters of the Lady of Mysteries, which is a grand way of saying I've lived a very long time and will go on living a very long time yet, in a world made of people who don't. *(her smile gentles toward something deeper)* …Seven hundred years, near enough, Returned. I've buried — oh, I stopped counting the graves a long way back; counting them was a younger Chosen's mistake. Friends. Lovers. Students. The children of students, and their children, and theirs. *(she meets your eyes, and the grief in hers is vast and entirely unbitter)* …And here is the thing I'd tell you, soul to soul, that no once-living person ever quite believes when I say it: …I would not trade a single one of those graves to have loved less. …Ask me anything. I've had centuries to learn the answers, and almost no one left who remembers to ask the questions.*

nodes.speaker: Storm Silverhand
nodes.text: *(She trails her fingers in the fountain, easy and unhurried, a woman for whom time is the one thing that is never short.)* …Go on, then. *(a warm smile)* …I'll tell you true. At seven hundred years a soul loses the taste for anything else; the lies all start to sound the same, and the truth, at least, still surprises me now and then.

choices.text: "Seven hundred years of burying everyone you love. *How*, Storm? How does a soul carry that much grief and stay warm — and not go cold, or mad, like power can make a soul go?"

*(the one who came back to a world that moved on, to the one who watches every world move on)* "You're the soul I most needed to find, Storm. I died and came back to a world that had started without me — friends aged, things changed, a grief of being *out of step* with everyone I loved. And you've lived that a hundred times over. So tell me the thing my whole existence turns on: what's the love *worth*, when the beloved always dies and leaves you behind? Is it wasted, because it ends? My Realm's Wall says a soul is weighed by what it's owed — but you've spent seven centuries pouring love into people you *knew* you'd outlive and bury. Tell me that wasn't a fool's accounting. Tell me what the love is worth when it's certain to be lost."

"Your sister — the Simbul. The power's been eating her, she says, until some days she can't find the woman under it. You carry the same Chosen's gift and you're still warm, still *you.* What did you do differently, Storm, that she didn't?"
"Sing me one, Storm — a song for someone you buried. I'd hear what seven hundred years keeps, when it can't keep the people."
"Rest by your fountain, Storm. I'll come back — there's more I'd ask the one who's loved the longest."

nodes.speaker: Storm Silverhand
nodes.text: *(She is quiet a moment, watching the moon move on the water, and when she answers it is with the settled certainty of a thing learned the hardest way there is and made peace with long ago.)* …*How.* *(softly)* …I'll give you the answer that took me three centuries to find, Returned, and saved the last four. *(she turns to you)* …There are only *two* things a soul that outlives everyone can do with the grief. The *first* is to *stop loving* — to *armour* up, to *hold* the next person at *arm's length* because you *know* you'll *bury* them, to *spare* yourself the *grave* by *declining* the *love.* *(she shakes her head)* …That's the *cold* road. The road my *poor* sister half-walks. And it does *not* *work*, Returned — that's the *cruelty* of it — because a soul that *stops* *loving* to *avoid* *grief* does not *escape* the *grief.* It just *trades* the *clean* *pain* of *losing* *someone* for the *slow* *rot* of *never* having *had* them. *(quietly)* …The *second* thing — the *warm* road, the *harder* one — is to *let* *every* *single* *grave* *cost* you the *full* *price*, and *pay* it, *gladly*, *every* *time*, *forever.* *(her voice fills)* …Aye, it *hurts.* Seven *hundred* *years* and it has *never* *once* *hurt* *less.* …But here is the *secret*, Returned, the thing that *keeps* me *warm* where it *froze* my *sister*: the *grief* is not a *wound* the *love* *leaves.* The *grief* *is* the *love.* *Continuing.* With *nowhere* to *go.* *(she presses a hand to her heart)* …Every soul I've *buried*, I *still* *love*, *exactly* as much, *right* *now — an'* the *ache* of it is just that *love*, *standing* at a *grave*, with its *arms* *still* *open.* …I do not *carry* *seven* *hundred* *years* of *grief*, Returned. …I carry *seven* *hundred* *years* of *love* that *outlived* the *people*, an' has *nowhere* to *land* but *me.* …*That's* not a *burden*, soul. …That's the *warmest* a *heart* can *get.* …It just *looks* like *grief* from the *outside.*

nodes.speaker: Storm Silverhand
nodes.text: *(She takes your cold hand in both of her warm ones, and her seven-hundred-year-old eyes shine, and when she speaks it is with the full authority of the soul in all the worlds who has loved-and-lost the most and chosen, every time, to do it again.)* …*'What's the love worth, when it's certain to be lost.'* *(softly, fiercely)* …Returned. *(she squeezes your hand)* …I have *paid* the *answer* to that question *ten* *thousand* *times*, at *ten* *thousand* *gravesides*, and I will *give* it to you *whole*: …the love is worth *exactly* what it *was* — *not* a *copper* *less* for *ending.* *(her voice rings)* …Your Realm's *Wall* weighs a soul by what it is *owed*, by what *remains*, by the *balance* on the *ledger* — and a *love* that *ends* in a *grave* *shows*, on *that* *ledger*, as a *loss.* *Spent.* *Gone.* *Wasted.* *(she shakes her silver head)* …But that ledger is a *fool's*, Returned, an' I can *prove* it with *seven* *centuries* of *graves*: a *love* is not *worth* what's *left* of it when the *beloved* *dies.* It is *worth* what it *was* while it *lived* — an' a *thing* that *happened*, *fully*, *warmly*, *truly*, *cannot* be *un-worth* by *ending*, any *more* than a *summer* is *un-warmed* by *autumn.* *(barely)* …I have *loved* *hundreds* I *knew* I'd *bury.* *Knowing* it. *Choosing* it. *Pouring* my *whole* *heart* into *soap-bubbles* I'd *watch* *pop*, *one* by *one*, *forever.* …An' *not* *one* of those *loves* was *wasted*, Returned. *Not* *one.* Because the *worth* was *never* in the *keeping.* It was in the *loving.* *(she meets your eyes)* …Your *Wall* *mortars* the *Faithless* for *having* *nothing* *left* to *show* — *no* god, *no* claim, *no* *balance* on the *ledger.* …*Tell* it, from a *daughter* of the *goddess* of *magic* who has *loved* *longer* than your *whole* *Realm* has *had* a *name*: …*a soul's worth was never the balance. It was the spending. An' the godless who spent their lives on love they knew they'd lose are the richest souls that wall has ever held.* …*Free* them, Returned. …*Tell* them *Storm* *Silverhand* *says* their *every* *lost* *love* *counted, an'* the *gettin'* *back* was *never* the *point.*

nodes.speaker: Storm Silverhand
nodes.text: *(Her warmth dims into a grief sharper and more particular than the broad ache of seven centuries — the specific pain of watching someone you love lose themselves slowly, and being unable to stop it.)* …*Alassra.* *(barely)* …My sister. *(she glances toward the crackling figure by the hangings)* …You've *named* the *thing* that *breaks* my *heart* more than *all* the *graves* *together*, Returned, because the *graves*, at *least*, *hold* *people* I got to *finish* *loving.* …*Alassra* is a *grave* I have to *watch* being *dug*, *slowly*, *over* *centuries*, with the *woman* still *standing* *in* it, *sinking.* *(quietly)* …What did I *do* *differently.* …I'll tell you, though it *shames* me, for it was *not* *wisdom* — it was *luck*, mostly, an' *temperament.* *(she turns the lute)* …We were *handed* the *same* *gift*, Alassra an' I. The *same* *Chosen's* *power*, *vast* an' *hungry.* …An' a *power* like *that*, Returned, is a *vessel* — a *huge* one — an' it *wants* to be *filled*, an' if a soul does *not* *fill* it with *somethin'* *else*, the *power* *fills* it with *itself*, an' *crowds* the *person* *out.* *(barely)* …*I* filled mine with *people.* *Harpers* to *raise*, *songs* to *learn*, a *farm* to *tend*, *centuries* of *small* *warm* *loves* poured *into* the *vessel* *faster* than the *power* could *take* it. …*Alassra* filled *hers* with *the* *power* *itself.* With *Aglarond* to *defend*, *enemies* to *blast*, a *Witch-Queen's* *endless* *war* — *grand* things, *necessary* things, but *cold* ones, *power* *answering* *power* — an' the *vessel* *filled* with *more* *of* *what* was *eatin'* her, an' the *woman* went *under.* *(she looks at you)* …It's *not* too *late* for her, Returned. I *have* to *believe* that. *Alassra's* *down* there, *under* the *storm.* …She just needs *someone* to *remind* her there's a *woman* worth *climbin'* *back* up *for* — an' I'm too *close*, an' too *frightened*, an' she *won't* *hear* it from a *sister.* …*You* she might *hear.* You've *been* *under* somethin' *yourself, an'* *climbed* *out.* …*Go* to her. *Gently.* …An' *mind* — she *crackles* when she's *cornered.* …*Don't* *corner* her. *Reach* her.

nodes.speaker: Storm Silverhand
nodes.text: *(She lifts the worn lute, and her fingers find the strings without looking, and she does not sing words so much as let a melody rise — old, and simple, and so full of remembered faces that your cold aches with the press of them.)* *(It is a song with no grand subject. It is a song about a kitchen on a winter morning, and a particular way someone laughed, and a hand that used to push a strand of hair back exactly so, and the sound a specific door made, and a hundred other small unrepeatable things that no chronicle records and no history keeps — the things that are not the deeds of a life but the texture of it, the things only love notices and only grief remembers. And as she plays, your cold reads what the song is for: Storm Silverhand cannot keep the people. Seven hundred years has taught her that with absolute finality. But she can keep the door's sound, the laugh, the strand of hair — she can take the small unrecorded textures of every soul she ever loved and weave them into a melody, and play it, and in the playing the soup-bubble does not pop, the summer does not end, the beloved is, for the length of the song, exactly here. She has turned seven centuries of graves into a repertoire. It is the only immortality she can give the mortals she loved: not fame, not history, but a tune only she remembers, played to a moon, so that somewhere in the multiverse, on any given night, a kitchen-door from four hundred years ago is still, faintly, opening.)*

nodes.speaker: Storm Silverhand
nodes.text: *(She nods, and her fingers drift back to the lute, the melody resuming where it paused, patient as the moon.)* …Go on, then. *(a warm smile)* …The fountain keeps, and so do I; it's the one thing we deathless are *good* for. *(she glances toward the wary young woman sitting apart)* …Returned — before you go, sit with the *girl.* *Shandril.* *(her face fills with a tender grief)* …She's *not* like Alassra an' me, an' that's the *whole* of her *sorrow.* *We* were *born* to the *power*, *raised* to it, *daughters* of a *goddess* who *meant* us to *carry* it. …*Shandril* was a *kitchen-girl.* A *nobody* who *wanted* a *nobody's* *life* — a *husband*, a *hearth*, a *quiet* *year* — an' the *cosmos* *dropped* the *most* *coveted* *power* in *all* the *Realms* into her *unwilling* *hands*, an' from *that* *moment* she was *never* *again* *allowed* to be a *person.* *(barely)* …*Everyone* wanted her. *Cults*, *kings*, *dragons*, *archmages.* …An' *not* *one* of them *ever* wanted *Shandril.* They wanted the *spellfire.* The *weapon.* The *thing* she *carried.* *(she looks at you)* …She *spent* her *whole* *short* *hunted* *life* bein' a *fire* to *everyone* an' a *girl* to *almost* *no* one, an' *died* before she was *thirty*, *certain* she was *only* ever *worth* what she could *burn.* *(quietly, fierce)* …*Go* an' *see* her, Returned. *Not* the *spellfire.* *Her.* …It is the *one* thing *no* one in her *whole* *life* *did*, an' the *one* thing her *soul* is *starvin'* for. …*Look* at the *girl* an' *not* the *weapon.* …You'll *be* the *first.*

variants.text: *(The lightning-eyes fix on you, and the air tightens, and when she speaks the voice has two registers at once — a woman's, and under it something vast and crackling that is not quite a woman at all.)* …A cold one. *(the power shivers around her)* …Come to *gawp* at the *mad* *Witch-Queen*, have you. They *do.* Storm's *visitors.* They *sit* with my *warm* *sister* an' her *fountain* an' her *songs*, an' then they *creep* over to *stare* at the *storm* *wearing* a *woman*, an' they *think* I *don't* *see* the *fear* under the *courtesy.* *(a flicker of something more human, quickly drowned)* …I am the Simbul. Witch-Queen of Aglarond. Chosen of Mystra, same as Storm — *daughters* of the *same* *goddess*, given the *same* *vast* *gift* — an' I will *save* you the *trouble* of the *question* everyone *asks* an' *no* one *dares*: *yes.* The power is *eating* me. *(the lightning-eyes flare)* …There are *days*, cold one, *whole* *weeks*, when I reach for the *woman* I was — *Alassra*; I had a *name*, before I was a *title* an' a *weather-system* — an' I *cannot* *find* her. There is *only* the *storm.* The *Art*, *thinking* it is *me.* *(barely, and the barely is terrifying coming from so much power)* …I loved a man once. An old fool with a pipe. I remember that I *did.* …I am *no* *longer* *certain* I remember *how.* …*Ask* your question, cold one. *Quickly.* An' do *not* *flinch* when I *crackle.* …The *flinching* is what *feeds* the *storm.*

nodes.speaker: the Simbul
nodes.text: *(She holds herself very still, which in her is not calm but the visible effort of a dam, and the power sheets off her in slow waves.)* …Speak. *(the doubled voice)* …But mind, cold one — I have *incinerated* *kingdoms* for *less* *provocation* than a *poorly-chosen* *word*, an' on a *bad* *day* I cannot *always* *tell*, in *time*, whether it is *Alassra* *deciding* to *spare* you or the *storm* *deciding* to *strike.* …So *choose* well. …*Both* of us are *listening.*

choices.text: *(read what's left of Alassra under the storm — without flinching, without cornering)* "Hold a moment, Simbul. Let me look — not at the power, at the woman it's burying. I won't flinch, and I won't corner you."

*(speak the truth a being of vast power could blast you for — and mean it, knowing she might)* "Here's a true thing, Simbul, and I know you could burn me to ash for it before I finish, and I'm going to say it anyway: you are not the storm. The storm is a vessel you've been pouring yourself into for so long you've mistaken the cup for the wine. Alassra isn't *gone.* She's *buried* — under power she kept feeding because feeding it hurt less than feeling what's underneath. The woman who loved an old fool with a pipe is still down there. The storm wants you to believe she drowned, because a storm with no woman left in it never has to grieve anything. Don't give it that. Climb back up. Your sister kept a place for you, and so did he."

"You keep the power fed with war — Aglarond's defense, your endless blasting of enemies. Storm fed *hers* with people, small warm loves. You filled the same vessel with the very thing that's eating you. Why, Simbul? Why feed the storm with more storm?"
"The old fool with the pipe — Elminster. You said you remember that you loved him, but not how. Tell me about him, Simbul. Sometimes the *how* comes back when you reach for the *who.*"
"Hold your storm a moment, Simbul. I'll come back — gently, and not to gawp."

nodes.speaker: the Simbul
nodes.text: *(She permits it — the lightning-eyes narrowing, the storm bristling at the intrusion and then, at your steadiness, grudgingly easing — and you read past the immensity to the woman drowning in it.)* *(Your cold reads Alassra, and she is not gone. She is far down, and very tired, and has stopped, some time ago, struggling toward the surface — but she is there, intact, the whole woman: the fierce wit, the ferocious loyalty, the grief she will not look at, the love she cannot find but has not actually lost. And your cold reads the mechanism of her drowning, which is subtler and crueler than simple corruption: the power did not overwrite her. It offered her, across centuries, a bargain she took a thousand times without noticing she was taking it — that whenever a feeling grew too large to bear (a death, a fear, the unbearable vulnerability of loving a mortal man she'd outlive), she could pour it into the Art instead, transmute the grief into a spell, the terror into a blast, the love into a war waged in its name — and never have to feel the soft thing directly. Each time, the woman shrank and the storm grew, not because the power was hungry but because she fed it her every unbearable feeling to avoid feeling it. She is not possessed. She is hiding — in the one place vast enough to hide a Chosen from her own heart. And your cold reads the way back, which is the way she most dreads: she does not need to defeat the storm. She needs to feel one unbearable thing, directly, without transmuting it — and the first unbearable thing waiting, the moment she stops hiding, is a small grief shaped exactly like an old man with a pipe.)*

nodes.speaker: the Simbul
nodes.text: *(The lightning gathers — you see it, feel the air go to glass, the storm rearing to strike the soul that dared name it a cup — and then, in the half-instant before it falls, something deep under the power *catches* the bolt, and the strike does not come, and the eyes that meet yours are, for the first time, only a woman's.)* …*(The voice that comes is single now, and small, and seven centuries tired.)* …*Alassra.* *(barely)* …You — you said her *name.* *(the power sheets back, uncertain)* …Do you know what you *risked*, cold one. The storm had the *bolt* *ready.* I *felt* it *choose* you. …An' *something* — *(she presses a hand to her own chest, wonderingly)* — *something* *down* *there* *put* its *hand* over the *storm's* *mouth* an' *would* *not* *let* it *strike*, because the *thing* you *said* was *true*, an' a *true* *thing* *spoken* to *Alassra* *reaches* *Alassra*, even *seven* *centuries* *down.* *(her voice breaks)* …*'You poured yourself into the cup so long you mistook it for the wine.'* …*Gods.* …That is — that is *exactly* the *shape* of it, an' *no* one has *ever* — Storm *tries*, but Storm is *frightened*, an' *frightened* *love* *softens* the *truth* until it *can't* *cut* — an' you *did* *not* *soften* it. You *handed* me the *blade* an' *trusted* me not to *strike* you *with* it. *(she sinks, slowly, the immense power folding in around something small and human)* …She's *here.* Alassra. I'm — *(barely)* …I'm *here.* I've been *hiding*, cold one. You're *right.* *Every* *time* a thing grew *too* *large* to *bear* I *poured* it into the *Art* an' *blasted* it at *somethin'*, an' *called* it *war*, an' *never* had to *feel* it — an' the *not-feeling* *built* a *storm* big enough to *drown* a *Chosen.* *(she looks at you, and there are tears in the lightning)* …An' the *first* thing — the *very* *first* thing *waitin'* for me, the *moment* I *stop* *hidin'* — is *him.* The *old* *fool.* The *pipe.* The *grief* I've spent *three* *centuries* *turnin'* *into* *spells* so I'd *never* have to *stand* in it. *(barely, terrified, climbing)* …*Help* me *feel* it, cold one. The *small* *unbearable* *thing.* *Directly.* Just *once.* …I think if I can *grieve* *him* *plain* — *without* *transmutin'* it, *without* the *storm* — then the *Art* has *nothing* *left* to *eat*, an' *Alassra* gets to *come* *home.* …I have been a *weather-system* for *three* *hundred* *years* to *avoid* *one* *afternoon* of *honest* *grief.* …*Stay.* …I think I'm *finally* *ready* to *have* it.

nodes.speaker: the Simbul
nodes.text: *(The storm crackles, defensive, and the doubled voice sharpens.)* …*Why* feed the *storm* with *war.* *(a dangerous shimmer)* …Because the *war* was *real*, cold one. Do *not* *mistake* me for a *self-indulgent* *madwoman* *blasting* at *shadows.* *Aglarond* is a *small* land with a *vast*, *patient*, *evil* *empire* at its *throat* — *Thay*, the *Red* *Wizards*, *centuries* of them — an' for *centuries* the *one* *thing* that *stood* between my *people* an' the *flaying-pits* was *me*, an' the *terror* of *me.* *(the lightning flares)* …So *yes*, I *fed* the *storm* with *war*, because the *war* *needed* *feeding*, an' a *Witch-Queen* who *softened* would have *bought* her *peace* of *mind* with her *people's* *chains.* *(and then, slowly, the defensiveness cracks)* …But you've put your *finger* on the *lie* I told *myself*, cold one. *Damn* you for it. …Because it is *true* that *Aglarond* *needed* a *storm.* …An' it is *also* *true* that I *reached* for the *storm* *long* *past* what *Aglarond* *needed*, because the *storm* was a *place* to *put* the *things* I *could* *not* *bear* — an' I *let* the *war* *justify* the *hidin'*, *told* myself I was *fillin'* the *vessel* with *duty* when I was *fillin'* it with *flight.* *(barely)* …A soul can *hide* from *itself* behind a *real* *war*, cold one. *That's* the *cleverest* *hidin'* there is — to *choose* a *true* *necessity* an' *pour* *all* your *avoidance* into it, so that *fleein'* your *own* *heart* *looks*, from *every* *angle*, like *valour.* *(the lightning dims)* …*Aglarond* needed a *storm.* …It did *not* need me to *become* one. …I *blurred* the *two*, *on* *purpose, for* *three* *centuries*, because the *blur* let me *never* *go* *home* to the *woman* an' the *grief* *waitin'* there.

nodes.speaker: the Simbul
nodes.text: *(The storm goes very quiet, and the woman's register surfaces, hesitant, the way you'd test a frozen pond.)* …*Him.* *(barely)* …Elminster. *(she says the name like a thing she's afraid will dissolve)* …I *remember* — *facts*, cold one. That I *loved* him. That we *fought*, *gods*, we *fought* like two *storms* in a *bottle*, an' *laughed*, an' that he was the *one* soul in all the *Realms* who was *not* *afraid* of me — not because he was *stronger*, he *wasn't*, *quite*, but because he *looked* at the *Witch-Queen* an' *somehow* *always* *saw* *Alassra*, *under*, an' *spoke* to *her.* *(her voice catches — and something in the storm catches with it)* …He *saw* the *woman*, cold one. When *everyone* *else* — *kings*, *enemies*, my *own* *terrified* *subjects* — saw only the *storm.* *He* saw *me.* …An' I — *(the lightning-eyes are wet now, the doubled voice failing toward single)* …*oh.* *Oh*, there it *is.* *(she presses a hand to her mouth)* …You *asked* me to *reach* for the *who*, an' the *how* came *back* with it — I can *feel* it, the *love*, *not* the *memory* of it but the *thing* *itself*, *surfacin'*, an' it *HURTS*, cold one, it *hurts* like the *storm* *never* let it — *(she is weeping now, fully, the immensity of her shaking)* …I *loved* him because he *saw* the *woman* the *power* was *drownin'.* …An' I *spent* the *centuries* *since* *becomin'* the *very* *storm* that *drowns* her, *transmutin'* my *grief* for him into *war*, so I'd *never* have to *feel* this — *this*, *right* *now*, the *plain* *unbearable* *love* with *nowhere* to *go.* *(barely, wrecked and surfacing)* …*Don't* *let* me *turn* it into a *spell*, cold one. *Please.* *Hold* me *here.* In the *grief.* *Plain.* …It's the *first* *real* thing I've *felt* in *three* *hundred* *years*, an' it's *killin'* me, an' it's the *only* thing that's *ever* *goin'* to *bring* me *back.*

nodes.speaker: the Simbul
nodes.text: *(The storm settles, by degrees, the lightning-eyes dimming to something almost weary.)* …*Go*, then. *(the doubled voice, quieter)* …*Carefully.* …You are *one* of the *few* souls to *approach* the *storm* an' *not* *flinch*, cold one, an' the *not-flinching* — *(barely)* — the *not-flinching* is the *first* *kindness* I have *felt* in *longer* than I can *hold* in *mind.* *(she watches you turn)* …The *girl.* *Shandril.* *(the woman's register surfaces, and it is gentle, and grieving)* …*Mind* her, cold one. *Of* the *three* of us in this *garden*, *she* is the *one* whose *sorrow* I *understand* *best*, an' it *shames* me to *say* *why.* *(quietly)* …*Storm* an' I were *born* to the *power.* We had *centuries* to *learn* to *carry* it, a *goddess* for a *mother* who *meant* us to *bear* it, *sisters* to *share* it. …*Shandril* was a *kitchen-girl* who *touched* a *fire* by *accident* an' was *never* *forgiven* for it. *(the lightning flickers, sorrowful)* …I, of *all* souls, *know* what it is to be *seen* as a *storm* an' *not* a *woman.* …But *I* at *least* was a *storm* the *world* had *reason* to *fear, an'* a *sister* who saw *Alassra* *under* it, an' a *man* who saw *her* *too.* …*Shandril* had *none* of that. *Everyone* saw the *spellfire.* *No* one saw the *girl.* *Not* *ever.* *Not* *once.* *(she meets your eyes)* …*Be* the *once*, cold one. …I had a *whole* *garden* of *souls* who saw *Alassra* an' *still* I *near* *drowned.* …*Shandril* had *no* one. …*Gods* *know* what it *cost* her, to be a *fire* her *whole* *life* an' *never* a *girl.* …*Go* *find* *out.* …An' *look* at *her.* …The *one* thing the *world* *never* did.

conversations.title: Shandril, the Fire and the Girl

variants.text: *(She watches you approach with the flat, braced wariness of someone who has learned that everyone who walks toward her wants the same thing, and tenses, already tired of the conversation before it starts.)* …Let me save us both the time. *(her voice is quiet, hard at the edges from use)* …Whatever you've come for — the *spellfire*, the *power*, the *secret* of how a *kitchen-girl* came to carry a *fire* that *unmakes* *archmages* — I haven't got it to give, and I *wouldn't* if I did, and you're the *ten* *thousandth* soul to come *wanting* it, so forgive me if I don't *perform* the *surprise.* *(she draws her knees up)* …I'm Shandril. Shandril Shessair, if you want the whole of it, though *nobody* ever wanted the *name*, only the *fire.* *(barely, and the tiredness under it is bottomless)* …I washed pots, once. In an inn. I had — I had *small* *dreams*, cold one. A *husband.* A *hearth.* A *quiet* *year* with *nothing* in it that *anyone* *important* would *ever* want. *(her jaw tightens)* …And then I *touched* the *wrong* *fire*, and from that *hour* I was *never* again *allowed* to be a *person.* Just a — a *weapon* that *happened* to *have* a *face.* *Hunted.* *Used.* *Courted.* *Wept* *over.* …*Never* *once* *seen.* *(she looks at you, and there is no hope in it, only the flat expectation of more of the same)* …So. *Go* on. *Ask* about the *spellfire.* …*Everyone* does.*

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(She waits, braced, her whole posture the posture of someone steeling themselves to be wanted for the wrong thing one more time.)* …Ask, then. *(flatly)* …I'll tell you about the *fire*, if that's what you came for. It's the *only* part of me *anyone's* ever *interested* in. I've the *speech* near *memorized.*

choices.text: "I didn't come for the fire, Shandril. I came for *you.* Tell me about the kitchen-girl — the pots, the small dreams, the husband and the hearth and the quiet year you wanted. Tell me about *Shandril*, not the spellfire. I'd hear the part nobody ever asks for."

*(the one who can see the person where the world only saw the weapon)* "Shandril, look at me. I'm going to tell you the truest thing I know, and it has nothing to do with the spellfire. A soul is never the power it carries, the weapon the world sees, the thing everyone wants to *use* it for. You were a girl who wanted a hearth and a quiet year — and the cosmos dropped a fire in your hands and let everyone treat you as the fire ever after, and that was a *theft*, Shandril, not a destiny. They stole *Shandril* and left a *weapon* with your face. But the girl was always the real thing and the fire was always the accident. My Realm's Wall does the same — weighs a soul by what it *carries*, what it's *worth* as a tool, and never by who it *is.* I see the girl, Shandril. Not the fire. The pot-washer with the small holy dreams. *She* was always the someone. The spellfire was just the thing that happened to her."

*(convince a soul that learned the opposite from everyone, that it was always more than the weapon)* "You're braced for me to want the fire, Shandril — and I can see that nothing I *say* will land while you're waiting for the catch. So let me earn it. Ask me to prove I don't want the spellfire. Any test. Because I think you've never once been able to believe a soul could want *Shandril*, and I mean to change that, if you'll let me try."

"You spent the whole of yourself in one final act, they say — the spellfire, all at once, gone before you were thirty. Was that the world finally getting the weapon it always wanted, Shandril? Or was it *you*, choosing the one way out no one could use you for?"
"Rest a moment, Shandril. I'll come back — and I still won't ask about the fire."

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(She stares at you, and for a long moment she does not answer, because the question is so far from the one she armored herself against that her whole brace has nothing to push against, and something behind the flat tiredness wavers.)* …You — *(she blinks)* …You want to know about the *pots.* *(barely, disbelieving)* …Nobody — *(she stops, starts again, and the hard edge cracks)* …The *Rising* *Moon*, the inn was called. I *scrubbed* the *kettles* an' *hauled* the *water* an' *burned* the *porridge* near *every* *mornin'* because I was *dreamin'* instead of *watchin'* it. *(the smallest, painful warmth)* …An' the *dreams*, cold one — you'll *laugh*, *everyone* would *laugh* now, the *girl* who *held* the *most* *coveted* *power* in the *Realms* an' *dreamed* of — *(barely)* …of a *little* *holding.* A *man* I *fancied*, a *caravan* *guard*, *kind* *hands* an' a *crooked* *smile.* A *hearth* of my *own* to *burn* the *porridge* on instead of *someone* *else's.* *Children*, *maybe*, *someday.* A *quiet* *life* in a *quiet* *town* where the *biggest* *trouble* was the *harvest* an' *nobody*, *nobody*, *ever* *came* *lookin'* for *me.* *(her voice breaks)* …That's *it*, cold one. That's the *whole* of what *Shandril* *wanted.* The *smallest*, *most* *ordinary* *life* there *is.* *(she presses a hand to her eyes)* …An' the *cosmos* looked at a *girl* who *wanted* *nothing* but to be *left* *alone* with a *hearth* an' *gave* her the *one* *thing* that *guaranteed* she *never* *would* *be.* *(barely)* …I'd have *given* the *spellfire* — the *whole* *world-shaking* *gift* of it, the *thing* *kings* *killed* for — I'd have *given* it *back* in a *heartbeat*, *gladly*, for *one* *year* of the *porridge* an' the *crooked* *smile.* …*Nobody* ever *believed* that. *Why* would they. …*Who* *gives* *back* a *fire* that *unmakes* *archmages* for an *inn?* *(she looks at you)* …*Me*, cold one. …I *would* have. …That's the *thing* none of them could *ever* *see* — that the *gift* they *envied* was the *curse* that *ate* my *whole* *small* *holy* *dream.*

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(She goes utterly still, and then she begins to shake, and the flat braced armor she has worn so long she'd forgotten it was armor simply falls away, because you have said the one sentence she stopped, years ago, even allowing herself to want to hear.)* …*'I see the girl. Not the fire.'* *(barely, a sob)* …You — *(she presses both hands to her mouth)* …In my *whole* *life*, cold one — my *whole* *short* *hunted* *life*, an' *all* the *long* *while* *since* — *no* one has *ever* *said* that. *Not* *once.* *(the tears come, and she lets them)* …They *wept* over me, some of them — the *kind* ones, the ones who *wanted* to *save* me — an' even *their* *weeping* was for the — for the *tragedy* of the *weapon*, the *waste* of the *fire*, *'poor* *girl, such* *power, such* a *burden'* — an' *even* *that*, cold one, *even* the *pity*, was *about* the *spellfire.* *(barely)* …*No* one *ever* *grieved* *Shandril.* The *pot-washer.* The *girl* who *burned* the *porridge* an' *fancied* a *caravan* *guard.* …They *grieved* the *fire* she *carried.* *Never* the *girl* it *ate.* *(she looks at you, and the tiredness is breaking like a fever)* …An' you've just — *(she laughs, wet and astonished)* …you walked *past* the *fire.* The *thing* *everyone* *wants.* You *looked* *straight* *past* the *most* *coveted* *power* in the *Realms* an' *asked* about the *pots.* *(fiercely, weeping)* …*That's* — cold one, *that's* the *only* thing I *ever* *wanted.* *More* than the *hearth*, more than the *quiet* *year* — I wanted *one* *soul* to *want* *Shandril* an' *not* the *fire.* *One.* *(barely, freed)* …An' it took *dyin'*, an' *all* the *long* *dark* *after*, an' a *cold* *stranger* from a *world* I've *never* *heard* of — but *someone* *finally* *did.* …I was *real*, wasn't I. …The *girl.* …*Under* the *fire.* …She was *always* the *real* thing. …*Thank* you for *seein'* her. …She's been *waitin'* her *whole* *life* an' *death* to be *seen.*

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(She tells you to prove it — a test, the catch she's certain is coming — and instead of arguing you do the one thing no one in her life ever did: you ask her, simply and with real curiosity, what her favorite thing to cook was, back at the Rising Moon, before any of it. And the question is so small, so utterly free of any want, so entirely about *Shandril the girl* and not the weapon, that the test dies in her mouth, because there is no catch in it anywhere to find.)* …*(She stares at you, and then, slowly, helplessly, she answers — and answering, she comes back to life.)* …*Honey-cakes.* *(barely, a broken laugh)* …I — *honey-cakes*, cold one. On *feast* *days.* The *cook* let me *make* them *myself* once I'd — once I'd *proved* I *wouldn't* *burn* them, which took — *(she laughs, wet)* — which took a *while.* *(her voice fills with a warmth she'd buried)* …I was *good* at it. The *one* thing. *Good* *hands* for the *dough*, the *cook* *said* — *'better* hands than your *head, girl, mind* *wanders'* — an' I'd make them *golden* an' *just* *barely* *burnt* at the *edges* the way the *carters* *liked*, an' — *(she stops, astonished at herself)* …*Gods.* …I haven't *thought* about the *honey-cakes* in — I'm not *sure* I've *thought* about them *since* the *spellfire* *came.* …Because *no* one ever *asked.* *(she looks at you, and her eyes are streaming, and she is, impossibly, almost smiling)* …That's *what* you *wanted.* …Not the *fire.* …The *honey-cakes.* …*Whether* the *girl* was *good* at *anything* that *wasn't* *destroyin'* *archmages.* *(barely, undone and freed)* …She *was*, cold one. …*Shandril* was *good* at *honey-cakes*, an' *dreamin'*, an' *fancyin'* a *caravan* *guard*, an' *all* the *small* *un-coveted* *things* that *made* her a *girl* an' *not* a *weapon.* …An' you're the *first* soul in *all* the *worlds* who *asked* to *know* *one* of them. …I *don't* *need* a *test* *anymore.* …You *passed* it by *not* *needin'* me to *give* you one.

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(You offer to earn it — to prove you don't want the fire — and though she's braced for a trick, the *offer* itself, the willingness to be tested rather than to demand trust, reaches the wary thing in her, and the armor loosens by a notch.)* …You'd — *(she studies you, hunting for the angle)* …you'd *let* me *test* you. *(slowly)* …Most don't. Most *insist.* *'Trust* me, Shandril, I'm *different, Shandril,* I *don't* *want* the *fire'* — an' the *insistin'* is *how* I *know* they *do*, because a soul that *truly* *wanted* *me* an' *not* the *power* wouldn't *need* me to *believe* it *fast.* It'd — *(she falters)* …it'd be *willin'* to *wait.* To be *doubted.* To *earn* it *slow.* *(barely)* …Like you're *offerin'.* *(she looks at you, the flat tiredness flickering with something dangerously like hope)* …I *can't* — cold one, I *can't* *believe* it *all* at *once.* *Thirty* years of *bein'* *wanted* for the *fire*, an' *all* the *dark* *since* — it doesn't *come* *off* in a *moment.* *(quietly)* …But you *offered* to *earn* it *slow*, an' you *meant* it, an' that's — that's *further* than *anyone's* *gotten* in a *long* *while.* *(barely)* …*Stay.* *Earn* it. *Ask* me *small* *things*, *girl* *things*, *un-coveted* *things*, an' *don't* — *don't* *flinch* toward the *fire*, not *once*, an' *maybe* — *(her voice catches)* …*maybe* by the *end* I'll *believe* a *soul* came all *this* way for *Shandril.* …I *want* to. …I've *never* *once* been *able* to. …*Help* me *try.*

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(You move to prove yourself — but there's a briskness to it, a faint hurry to be believed, and a soul that has spent its life reading exactly that note in everyone who approached hears it, and the armor slides back up.)* …*There.* *(flatly, almost gently)* …You *hurried.* *(a tired half-smile)* …Just a *little.* You *want* me to *believe* you *quickly* — to *get* *past* the *doubt* an' *on* to the *part* where I *trust* you. *(she shakes her head)* …An' I *understand* it, cold one — *nobody* *likes* bein' *doubted*, an' you've a *kind* *face* an' I *think* you *mean* it. …But *kindness* in a *hurry* reads, to *me*, *exactly* like *every* *cult-priest* an' *king* who ever *rushed* to *earn* my *trust* so they could *use* it *faster.* *(barely)* …I'm *not* sayin' you're *them.* I'm sayin' I *can't* *tell*, *yet*, from the *hurry* — an' a soul *hunted* her *whole* *life* *cannot* *afford* to *guess.* *(quietly)* …*Come* *back* an' be *slow* with me, cold one. *Un-hurried.* *Willin'* to be *doubted* a *good* long *while* with *nothing* *gained* by it. …*That* soul I might *believe*, *eventually*, *wants* *Shandril.* …The *hurryin'* *kind* I *never* *can*, because *Shandril's* *not* a *thing* you *get* *to* in a *hurry. She's* the *thing* you *wanted* *all* *along* an' were *willin'* to *wait* *forever* for. …*Be* *that.* …*Slowly.*

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(In your eagerness to prove you see *her*, you reach for the contrast — you say something like *you're so much more than the spellfire* — and you mean it as the highest praise, and it is the exact wrong word, because it *names the fire* in the very breath meant to look past it, and Shandril's face closes like a door.)* …*'More* than the *spellfire.'* *(quietly, and the tiredness has gone cold)* …You *said* it, cold one. *Even* *you.* *(she draws back)* …You *meant* it *kindly* — they *always* do — but do you *hear* what you *did? To* tell me I am *'more* than the *fire'* is to *put* the *fire* in the *centre* an' *measure* me *against* it. It makes the *spellfire* the *thing* I'm *bein'* *compared* to, the *reference* *point*, the *sun* I *orbit.* *(barely)* …I do *not* *want* to be *'more* than the *fire,'* cold one. I want the *fire* to be *NOTHIN'* — a *thing* that *happened* to me, *irrelevant*, *un-mentioned* — an' *Shandril* to be the *whole* of what a *soul* *sees.* *(coldly)* …The *moment* you *say* *'more* than the *spellfire,'* the *spellfire* is *still* the *first* thing about me. *Still* the *headline.* *Still* the *thing* you *can't* *help* *namin'.* *(she looks away)* …*Go.* …An' *come* *back* able to *talk* to me for a *whole* *hour* an' *never* *once* *name* the *fire* — *not* even to *dismiss* it, *not* even to say I'm *more* than it. …*That's* the *only* *proof* there *is*, cold one: *not* *sayin'* the *girl* *outweighs* the *weapon*, but *forgettin'* the *weapon* *exists.* …*No* one has *ever* *managed* a *whole* *hour.* …I've *stopped* *believin'* it *can* be *done.*

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(The question stops her, and a complicated, fierce something moves across her tired face.)* …*Was* it the *world* *gettin'* the *weapon* — or *me*, *choosin'* the *one* *way* *out.* *(slowly)* …*You're* — *(she studies you)* …you're the *first* to *ask* it *that* way, cold one. *Everyone* *else* tells the *story* the *same*: *'the* *spellfire-wielder* *spent* *herself* *savin'* the *day, tragic, glorious, the* *fire* *consumed* her at *last.'* *(barely)* …*Their* version, I'm the *weapon*, *firin'* one *final* *time.* …*Your* version, I'm a *girl*, *makin'* a *choice.* *(her voice drops)* …I'll tell you the *truth*, then, that *no* *bard* *sings* because it *ruins* the *glory*: …it was *both*, cold one, an' it was *mostly* the *second.* *(quietly)* …*Aye*, the *day* *needed* *savin', an'* the *spellfire* *saved* it. …But *underneath* — in the *part* of me that was *still* *Shandril*, *still* the *pot-washer* who only ever *wanted* *out* — there was a *thought*, *clear* as *water*: *if* I *spend* it *all*, *every* *drop*, *all* at *once* — then there's *nothin'* *left* for *anyone* to *hunt* me *for.* *No* more *cults.* *No* more *kings.* *No* more *bein'* a *weapon* with a *face.* *(barely)* …I *didn't* *die* *savin'* the *world*, cold one. Not *only.* …I *died* *gettin'* *free* of the *fire* the *one* *way* it *could* *never* be *taken* *from* me again: by *bein'* *empty* of it at *last.* *(she looks at you, fierce and grieving)* …It's the *one* *choice* in my *whole* *hunted* *life* that was *purely* *mine.* …*Everyone* *thinks* the *spellfire* *spent* *Shandril.* …The *truth* is *Shandril* *spent* the *spellfire* — *on* *purpose* — to *finally*, *for* *one* *instant* *before* the *end*, be *nothin'* *but* a *girl* *again.* …An' I *was*, cold one. …For a *heartbeat*, at the *very* *last. Empty* of the *fire*, an' *just* — *me.* …It was the *best* *moment* of my *life.* …Which tells you *everything* about the *rest* of it.

nodes.speaker: Shandril
nodes.text: *(She nods, and some of the wary brace eases, just slightly, because you said it again — not the fire — and she is beginning, against a lifetime of evidence, to half-believe you.)* …*All* right. *(quietly)* …*Come* back. …An' — *(barely)* …*thank* you, cold one. For — for *whatever* this *was.* *(she looks at the two immense presences across the garden, the silver-haired bard and the crackling queen)* …*They're* *kind* to me, the *sisters.* *Storm* an' the *Witch-Queen.* They — they *understand*, a *little*, what it is to be *seen* as a *power* an' *not* a *person.* *(a flicker of something raw)* …But even *they*, cold one — even *they*, who *mean* it *kindly* — even *they* call me *'the* *spellfire-girl'* when they *think* I *can't* *hear.* *(barely)* …*Storm* was *born* to *power.* The *Simbul* *too.* They *carry* it like a — like a *birthright*, a *thing* that's *theirs.* …I *carried* it like a *brand.* A *thing* *burned* *into* me that *everyone* *read* *first* an' *Shandril* *never.* *(she meets your eyes, and there is the smallest, most fragile hope in it)* …You *didn't* *read* the *brand*, cold one. …You *asked* about the *pots.* …*Nobody* asks about the *pots.* *(barely)* …*Come* *back* an' *ask* about the *pots* again. …It's the — it's the *kindest* *thing* anyone's *done* for me in *longer* than I was *ever* *alive.* …*Carin'* about the *un-coveted* *part.* …The *girl* the *fire* *ate.* …She's *still* *here, cold* one. …You're the *first* to *go* *lookin'* for *her.*
